test_description: For each masked nside=256 temperature map, remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels, set masked pixels to zero, transform to spherical harmonics, retain only multipoles 2 <= l <= 30, and synthesize the corresponding low-l map. On the unmasked pixels of this filtered map, compute the single scalar excess kurtosis K = <(T - <T>)^4> / <(T - <T>)^2>^2 - 3, with averages taken over unmasked pixels only. Compare the Planck SMICA value of K to the distribution of K from the 1000 identically processed LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: The Planck SMICA masked low-l excess kurtosis K is anomalously different from the LCDM simulation distribution, either unusually high or unusually low.
justificaton: The code and description consider both directions: high K indicates unusually heavy-tailed or peaked low-l structure, while low K indicates unusually platykurtic structure. Since no clear prior directional preference is specified and the summary reports both upper/lower-tail and two-sided p-values, the appropriate default is a two-tailed test.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Low-l Excess Kurtosis: remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels of each masked nside=256 temperature map, zero-fill the mask, transform to spherical harmonics, keep only 2 <= ell <= 30, synthesize the corresponding low-ell map, and compute the unmasked-pixel excess kurtosis K = <(T-<T>)^4>/<(T-<T>)^2>^2 - 3.
custom_summary.statistic: Excess kurtosis K = <(T-<T>)^4>/<(T-<T>)^2>^2 - 3 of the masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted, 2<=ell<=30 synthesized map, evaluated on unmasked pixels only
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail for unusually heavy-tailed/peaked low-ell temperature structure; lower tail for unusually platykurtic low-ell structure
custom_summary.monopole_dipole_treatment: best-fit monopole plus Cartesian dipole removed on unmasked pixels before zero-filling the mask and harmonic filtering
custom_summary.mask_treatment: input finite pixels define the binary analysis mask; masked NaN pixels are zero-filled for harmonic transforms, while kurtosis averages use only unmasked pixels

plot_spec.title: Masked low-l excess kurtosis
plot_spec.xlabel: K = excess kurtosis of 2<=ell<=30 masked low-l map
plot_spec.caption: LCDM simulation distribution of the masked low-l excess-kurtosis statistic. Planck is compared to identically processed simulations; larger K indicates heavier tails or a more peaked unmasked low-l temperature distribution.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail for the masked low-l excess-kurtosis statistic.

test_summary.Description: Excess kurtosis K was computed on unmasked pixels of masked, monopole/dipole-subtracted Planck SMICA and 1000 LCDM maps after retaining only 2 <= l <= 30.
test_summary.Results: Planck has K = 0.255, compared with simulation mean 0.0058, median -0.0157, and std 0.203. It lies at about the 89th percentile, with z ≈ 1.23 and two-tailed p = 0.218.
test_summary.Interpretation: The Planck value is mildly high but well inside the LCDM simulation distribution and not in either 5% tail. This is not evidence for anomalous low-l non-Gaussianity by this kurtosis statistic.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Broadly consistent with Planck Collaboration, “Planck 2015 results. XVI. Isotropy and statistics of the CMB” (arXiv:1506.07135), which reports skewness/kurtosis-based Gaussianity tests generally consistent with simulations, while noting specific localized features such as the Cold Spot.
